---
title: "Jan Averkin"
subtitle: "Doctoral researcher in economics · ETH Zürich"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 13em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:averkinjan@gmail.com      # TODO: swap for your ETH email
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/janaverkin
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/        # TODO: your Scholar profile URL
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/          # TODO: your LinkedIn URL
---

Welcome! I'm a doctoral researcher in economics at **ETH Zürich**. My work sits
at the intersection of **development economics**, **social protection**, and the
**economics of child labour**, with a regional focus on West Africa.

I study how households in low-income, agricultural settings respond to policy —
in particular how cash transfers reshape children's work, schooling, and
household decision-making. My current research evaluates the effects of an
unconditional cash transfer on child labour in cocoa-farming households in Ghana.

Beyond research, I occasionally write here about things I'm working on, reading,
or building. Take a look at the [Research](research.qmd) page for an overview of
my projects, the [Blog](blog.qmd) for posts, or my [CV](cv.qmd).

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This site is a work in progress — content is still being filled in.
:::
